summary: Current gas ~$2.87, crude at $64 WTI. EIA forecasts $2.92 annual avg. Linear WTI-gas extrapolation gives $2.90 for March. Seasonal uptick expected but lower crude keeps prices subdued vs 2024-2025.
content:
Key data points:
- Jan 2026 monthly avg: $2.809 (FRED GASREGM)
- Weekly: $2.867 as of Feb 2, 2026 (rising from $2.779 low)
- AAA: $2.89 as of Feb 5, 2026
- WTI crude: $64.29 (vs EIA forecast of $52.21)
- EIA STEO annual forecast: $2.92/gal for 2026
- March 2025: $3.096 (WTI ~$70)
- March 2024: $3.426 (WTI ~$80)
- Implied WTI-gas slope: ~$0.033/gal per $1 WTI
- At $64 WTI → ~$2.90 for March
- Normal Jan-to-Mar increase: avg +$0.27 (but wide range: +$0.02 to +$0.53)
- Iran tensions could spike crude to $91 (BNEF scenario)
